weekly_template.name: AI Recommended — Max Boyatt — Power — 2026-06-30
weekly_template.description: AI-generated weekly program. Focus: Power. Source: ai_layer.recommended_programs id=13.

activity_templates.name: Prep — Max Boyatt
activity_templates.description: Max's prep focuses on addressing deficits in T-spine/shoulder mobility (related to elbow flexion), hip/pelvis control, and lower body power. The selected exercises prioritize dynamic hip mobility, glute/core activation, and movement integration to prime his body for high-force output.

exercises.exercise_name: Pull Back Butt Kick to Walking Spiderman w/ Hip Lift and Reach
exercises.matched_exercise_name: Pull Back Butt Kick to Walking Spiderman w/ Hip Lift and Reach
exercises.notes: To dynamically warm up the entire body, improve hip mobility, and initiate T-spine rotation.

exercises.exercise_name: All Fours Band T Spine Rotation
exercises.matched_exercise_name: All Fours Band T Spine Rotation
exercises.notes: To improve thoracic spine rotation, which can indirectly support better elbow mechanics and reduce compensation.
To enhance hip external rotation mobility, crucial for optimal pelvic mechanics and power transfer.
To improve hip internal and external rotation control, directly impacting pelvic rotation and stability.

exercises.exercise_name: Single Leg Hip Thrust Off Wall
exercises.matched_exercise_name: Single Leg Hip Thrust Off Wall
exercises.notes: To activate the glutes and improve single-leg stability, foundational for powerful lower body force production.

exercises.exercise_name: 90/90 Hip Lift w/ Right Arm Reach
exercises.matched_exercise_name: 90/90 Hip Lift w/ Right Arm Reach
exercises.notes: To activate deep core stabilizers and control pelvic position, supporting efficient force transfer and deceleration.
To activate the serratus anterior for improved scapular stability, supporting overall shoulder health and power.
To dynamically load and integrate the posterior chain in a single-leg stance, preparing for powerful athletic movements.
To integrate core stability with dynamic rotational movement, enhancing neuromuscular control and power output for a power-focused athlete.

activity_templates.name: Bulletproofing — Max Boyatt
activity_templates.description: Max's bulletproofing focuses on improving shoulder range of motion and end-range control, particularly addressing the deficit in elbow flexion at maximum external rotation. The selected exercises prioritize mobility, posterior capsule health, and isometric stability to support overall shoulder resilience.

exercises.notes: This exercise builds isometric strength at end-range, crucial for improving control and stability of the posterior capsule and external rotation (per side).
Targets posterior capsule tightness to improve internal rotation and overall glenohumeral joint mobility (per side).
Improves anterior shoulder and thoracic mobility, contributing to better overall throwing mechanics and cuff health.
Enhances thoracic spine and shoulder mobility with dynamic control, supporting scapular stability during throwing motions (per side).

activity_templates.name: Plyo — Max Boyatt
activity_templates.description: This three-day plyo cycle for Max Boyatt, a college starter in the Velocity Phase, is designed to address his primary pitching mechanics deficits: insufficient elbow flexion at max external rotation, delayed pelvis rotation stop, and low lead leg impulse. The Velocity Phase allows for high-intent work, so no downgrades from the recommended plyo levels (P0, P1, P2) are necessary. The cycle strategically integrates drills across intensities, with P0 prioritizing tissue recovery and feel, P1 balancing feel with light intent to build patterns, and P2 aggressively targeting his inefficiencies with maximal intent and movement correction. The weekly layout balances work and recovery, crucial for a starter maintaining high velocity in this phase.

sessions.label: Recovery day
sessions.session_intent: To promote tissue recovery and restore foundational movement feel post-outing, with a gentle emphasis on arm path and hip mobility without spiking intent.

drills.notes: Uses a heavy ball to prepare tissue while encouraging a full, fluid arm path and trunk rotation.
Focuses on controlled hip rotation and separation, gently addressing the pelvis rotation deficit.
Promotes a clean arm path and shoulder mobility, aiding recovery and feel for the elbow flexion deficit.
Encourages lead leg engagement and hip-first movement, providing a low-intensity stimulus for lead leg impulse.
Reinforces layback and arm deceleration mechanics with a heavy ball, beneficial for elbow health and arm path feel.

sessions.label: Hybrid day
sessions.session_intent: To bridge recovery and work, introducing moderate intent to reinforce efficient movement patterns for arm path, hip drive, and lead leg action.

drills.notes: Builds on recovery, encouraging fluid body-arm connection and preparatory arm speed.
Increases focus on active pelvis rotation and separation for improved kinematic sequencing.
Emphasizes dynamic lead leg engagement and timing, addressing the lead leg impulse deficit.
Promotes an efficient arm path and internal rotation, directly aiding the elbow flexion deficit.
Connects momentum to hip drive and trunk rotation, assisting the pelvis rotation stop time.
Continues to refine arm path and rhythm in a controlled, body-aware manner.

sessions.label: Work day
sessions.session_intent: To express maximal intent and aggressively correct Max's movement inefficiencies, focusing on lead leg block, hip-shoulder separation, and optimal arm path for velocity gains.

drills.notes: Establishes full body connection and momentum for high-intent throwing.
Drives powerful hip-shoulder separation to improve pelvis rotation stop timing.
Trains an aggressive lead leg block and ground force absorption, directly targeting the lead leg impulse deficit.
Encourages a full layback and arm whip through maximal external rotation, correcting elbow flexion.
Focuses on an explosive hip rotation and trunk acceleration for improved energy transfer and pelvis stop timing.
Promotes dynamic lead leg drive and hip-to-shoulder separation, crucial for lead leg impulse and pelvis rotation.
Enhances arm path efficiency and fluidity through the throwing motion, addressing optimal elbow flexion.
